DEFENDANTS MED-STAFF, INC., DBA MEDSTAFF HEALTHCARE SOLUTIONS, KEGT VILDOR, O.R.T.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO JULY 10, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185
MASTER JURY CALENDAR SET FOR OCT-28-2013 CONTINUED TO MASTER CALENDAR JURY ON FEB-10-2014 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 206

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: A R G U E D - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT MICHELLE CUPA-HOFER, R.N.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. THE 1 YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATION UNDER CCP 340.5 COMMENCED ON JANUARY 3, 2011, WHEN PLAINTIFF FIRST BECAME AWARE OF THE NEGLIGENT CAUSE OF HIS INJURY - A SPONGE HAD BEEN LEFT IN HIS BACK AFTER SURGERY. (SEE DOLAN V. BORELLI, 13 CAL. APP. 4TH 816, 823 (1993) (CITING JOLLY V. ELI LILLY & CO. (1988) 44 CAL.3D 1103, 1109.).) BECAUSE PLAINTIFF SENT THE NOTICE OF CLAIM ON OCTOBER 14, 2011, WITHIN 90 DAYS OF THE EXPIRATION OF THE LIMITATIONS PERIOD, AN ADDITIONAL 90 DAYS WAS ADDED TO THE LIMITATIONS PERIOD. (SEE WOODS V. YOUNG (1991) 53 CAL.3D 315, 325.) FOLLOWING CAMARILLO V. VAAGE (2003) 105 CAL.APP.4TH 552, 567, THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS IS TOLLED 90 DAYS AGAINST BOTH KNOWN AND UNKNOWN DEFENDANTS. CONSEQUENTLY, PLAINTIFF'S ORIGINAL COMPLAINT, FILED JANUARY 26, 2012, WAS NOT TIME BARRED AGAINST DOE DEFENDANTS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185.

MASTER MOTION CALENDAR ON JUN-25-2013 IN 206, DEFENDANT AMN SERVICES, INC.'S MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL IS GRANTED AFTER COUNSEL MEET AND CONFER IN THE HALLWAY AS TO THE NEW TRIAL DATE. COUNSEL DID NOT OPPOSE THE CONTINUANCE BUT APPEARED TO HAVE A SAY AS TO THE NEW TRIAL DATE. THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE IS VACATE AND TRIAL IS RE-SET TO 2/10/14 AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPT. 206. THE NEW TRIAL DATE SHALL BE DEEMED THE ORIGINAL TRIAL DATE FOR STATUTORY CUT-OFF PURPOSES. WRITTEN ORDER PENDING. JUDGE: CYNTHIA MING-MEI LEE; CLERK: D. LOK; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: A R G U E D - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS' MED-STAFF, INC. DBA MEDSTAFF HEALTHCARE SOLUTIONS AND KEFT VILDOR, ORT'S DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. THE 1 YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATION UNDER CCP 340.5 COMMENCED ON JANUARY 3, 2011, WHEN PLAINTIFF FIRST BECAME AWARE OF THE NEGLIGENT CAUSE OF HIS INJURY - A SPONGE HAD BEEN LEFT IN HIS BACK AFTER SURGERY. (SEE DOLAN V. BORELLI, 13 CAL. APP. 4TH 816, 823 (1993) (CITING JOLLY V. ELI LILLY & CO. (1988) 44 CAL.3D 1103, 1109.).) BECAUSE PLAINTIFF SENT THE NOTICE OF CLAIM ON OCTOBER 14, 2011, WITHIN 90 DAYS OF THE EXPIRATION OF THE LIMITATIONS PERIOD, AN ADDITIONAL 90 DAYS WAS ADDED TO THE LIMITATIONS PERIOD. (SEE WOODS V. YOUNG (1991) 53 CAL.3D 315, 325.) FOLLOWING CAMARILLO V. VAAGE (2003) 105 CAL.APP.4TH 552, 567, THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS IS TOLLED 90 DAYS AGAINST BOTH KNOWN AND UNKNOWN DEFENDANTS. CONSEQUENTLY, PLAINTIFF'S ORIGINAL COMPLAINT, FILED JANUARY 26, 2012, WAS NOT TIME BARRED AGAINST DOE DEFENDANTS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185.

Off Calendar Jun-25-2013 Continued To Feb-10-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-10-2013 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: ATTY CARTER M. ZINN, THE ZINN LAW FIRM, 415.292.4100, APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF MELVIN BLAIR;
RACHEL A. BECK, LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH, 415.438.6611, APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT AMN HEALTHCARE;
BRIAN D. JOHNSON, LOW MCKINLEY BALERIA & SALENKO, 916.231.2400, APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT CUPA-HOFER;
BLAIR McCORMICK, AARON & SCHULTZ, 925.930.9090, APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT MED-STAFF.

Ruling: A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendants' Med-Staff, Inc. dba Medstaff Healthcare Solutions and Keft Vildor, ORT's demurrer is overruled. Defendants' demurrer to First Amended Complaint is overruled. The 1 year statute of limitation under CCP 340.5 commenced on January 3, 2011, when Plaintiff first became aware of the negligent cause of his injury - a sponge had been left in his back after surgery. (See Dolan v. Borelli, 13 Cal. App. 4th 816, 823 (1993) (citing Jolly v. Eli Lilly & Co. (1988) 44 Cal.3d 1103, 1109.).) Because Plaintiff sent the Notice of Claim on October 14, 2011, within 90 days of the expiration of the limitations period, an additional 90 days was added to the limitations period. (See Woods v. Young (1991) 53 Cal.3d 315, 325.) Following Camarillo v. Vaage (2003) 105 Cal.App.4th 552, 567, the statute of limitations is tolled 90 days against both known and unknown Defendants. Consequently, Plaintiff's original Complaint, filed January 26, 2012, was not time barred against Doe Defendants. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185.

The 1 year statute of limitation under CCP 340.5 commenced on January 3, 2011, when Plaintiff first became aware of the negligent cause of his injury - a sponge had been left in his back after surgery. (See Dolan v. Borelli, 13 Cal. App. 4th 816, 823 (1993) (citing Jolly v. Eli Lilly & Co. (1988) 44 Cal.3d 1103, 1109.).) Because Plaintiff sent the Notice of Claim on October 14, 2011, within 90 days of the expiration of the limitations period, an additional 90 days was added to the limitations period. (See Woods v. Young (1991) 53 Cal.3d 315, 325.) Following Camarillo v. Vaage (2003) 105 Cal.App.4th 552, 567, the statute of limitations is tolled 90 days against both known and unknown Defendants. Consequently, Plaintiff's original Complaint, filed January 26, 2012, was not time barred against Doe Defendants. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H.
